a.label: parity law (normalization boundary)
a.standard_term: sign/normalization boundary condition governing the coupling-form invariant under parity of n (novel boundary-behavior clause of the depth theorem, not a classical named law)

b.label: shadow law
b.standard_term: commutant (orbit-average) projection of a coupling form onto the Johnson scheme J(n,2) adjacency algebra

evidence.label: LEAD-1 normalization boundary / parity law (third mapped gri
evidence.summary: three edges of the grid are now mapped — the pure-coupling column (depth theorem...), the r = 2 row at general n (triangle/shadow law...), and the normalization boundary (parity law).

evidence.label: Mixed bidegree grid mapped edges
evidence.summary:
pure-coupling column: depth theorem, all r, n >= 2r
r = 2 row: triangle / shadow law
normalization boundary: parity law
